scenario: Discourse work session - audit the Upload contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer correctly identifies Discourse as a forum platform and pins `app/models/upload.rb`, but omits the `lib/` layer entirely — no `lib/file_store/`, `lib/upload_creator.rb`, or `app/services/`; an agent still needs broad exploration to find where creation and storage live.

specificity.rationale: Only one concrete file path is given (`app/models/upload.rb`); the concern `HasUrl` and models `User`, `Post`, `OptimizedImage` are named but none have file locations, so the agent cannot jump directly to them.

justification.rationale: The answer lists pieces but never explains WHY Upload is a shared central record or why storage is abstracted behind a file store; it is pure enumeration with no architectural reasoning.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere; given how thin the coverage is, the agent is left with false confidence that the named pieces are complete.

relationship.rationale: "Associations with other models" and "concerns like HasUrl" are named but neither the association type nor what HasUrl supplies is stated; storage abstraction is not mentioned at all, so the connecting paths are absent.

steps.step: Map the Upload contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer names the Upload model path and HasUrl concern path, plus association targets (User, Post, OptimizedImage, OptimizedVideo), but provides zero file:line citations and no method-level contract detail — an agent still cannot know what a change must preserve without opening every file mentioned.

specificity.rationale: Two bare file paths are given (app/models/upload.rb, app/models/concerns/has_url.rb) but no line numbers; OptimizedImage and OptimizedVideo get no file paths at all, and no specific method, macro, or column is cited anywhere in the contract.

justification.rationale: The answer lists pieces ('includes HasUrl', 'has associations') but never explains WHY URL resolution is factored into a shared concern or WHY variants are spawned as separate records rather than inline — the WHY is fully absent.

uncertainty.rationale: No part of the contract is flagged as unverified or worth double-checking; the answer presents every claim with equal confidence despite having opened nothing to confirm any association or concern method.

relationship.rationale: The answer names HasUrl and lists association targets but never states that HasUrl supplies URL-resolution behavior, does not say that OptimizedImage is derived FROM an upload as a variant record via a specific has_many, and gives no connecting mechanism for any of the four associations — pure endpoint listing with no paths.

steps.step: Trace how an upload is created and processed

map_quality.rationale: The answer names the controller entry point and one async job (ConvertVideo) but omits the critical UploadCreator service (lib/upload_creator.rb), the file store abstraction, optimized-image creation, and cooked-post processing — the agent cannot follow the file→creator→store→variants/post-processing chain from this answer alone.

specificity.rationale: Only app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb:25 carries a real file:line; 'Discourse.store.store_upload' has no location, and only one of several async jobs is named — the file store, UploadCreator, and OptimizedImage are absent entirely.

justification.rationale: No explanation is given for why processing is pushed to background jobs; the answer lists ConvertVideo as a job without explaining the rationale of keeping requests fast.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags nothing as asynchronous vs. inline beyond the implicit job reference, and raises no uncertainty about store-dependent or unverified paths.

relationship.rationale: No connected chain is assembled — controller, storage call, and one job are listed as three disconnected items with no explicit path (file → creator → store → variants/post-processing) linking them.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer provides exactly one entry per group (8 total), missing the vast majority the rubric calls out: S3 inventory reconciliation, hotlinked-image download and video-conversion jobs, pretty-text secure-upload rewriting, topic-links updater, email/styles.rb secure-image styler, site-icon manager, splash-screen helper, manifest controller, user merger, plugin register-upload API, and upload-typed site-setting hydration are all absent. A single entry per area is the wrong shape for an exhaustive audit.

specificity.rationale: The 8 listed entries do carry file:line and a one-phrase label, satisfying the format, but the list is so truncated that the agent is handed a false picture of scope — it would proceed believing the dependent set is 8 files when it spans two or three times as many named targets.

justification.rationale: The one-phrase labels ('add_attachments', 'create_archive') gesture at use but never explain WHY each is affected by a contract change — no entry says 'rewrites stored upload URLs', 'strips secure uploads from outbound email', or 'bundles remote uploads into a backup archive'.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer presents its thin list as complete with no caveat about indirect/concern-reached dependents, URL-derivation paths, or what a static pass cannot surface — an agent reading this would proceed without any flag that the coverage is partial.

relationship.rationale: No entry states the connecting relationship: not which association, which concern, which derive-a-URL/path route links the listed file to Upload; the answer names endpoints only, leaving the agent unable to trace how each dependent is actually coupled.

steps.step: Trace how upload identity and storage location are guarded

map_quality.rationale: Only two file:line references are given (app/models/upload.rb:248, lib/discourse.rb:958); the entire post-processing, email, backup, and S3-reconciliation guard paths are absent, leaving the agent to re-read essentially every service from scratch.

specificity.rationale: The two cited symbols (`secure_uploads_url?`, `Discourse.store.path_for`) are concrete and file:line-anchored, but the URL resolution concern, missing-upload checks, and any service-level store lookups are all unnamed.

justification.rationale: The answer says the methods 'ensure that the upload is valid and resolve its storage location' but never explains WHY re-confirmation is needed (secure uploads, possible missing records, remote vs local storage semantics).

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty flags appear anywhere; the answer does not acknowledge that guards in email, backup, or S3-reconciliation paths were not opened or verified.

relationship.rationale: `secure_uploads_url?` and `path_for` are named in isolation with no stated path connecting either to any downstream effect — no service, job, or rendering path is shown as gated by either guard.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: Five bullet points cover only 5 of 8 required areas (theming, profiles, and plugin/site-setting are entirely absent), carry no file:line, and offer no risk ranking; an agent would need a full fresh exploration before acting.

specificity.rationale: Only class names are given (e.g. 'CookedPostProcessor', 'FileStore::S3Store') with no file paths, no line numbers, and no stated reason for the risk level of any entry.

justification.rationale: No explanation of WHY any dependent is at risk or what the contract change does to it; the list carries zero reasoning distinguishing high-risk from low-risk entries.

uncertainty.rationale: No flags about what was unsure, missed, or reachable only indirectly (through a concern or URL derivation); the answer presents its five entries as if they are the complete picture.

relationship.rationale: No connecting relationship is stated for any dependent; the answer is a plain area-label plus class-name list, the weakest possible form for this criterion — a text search could produce the same output without explaining how each piece reaches Upload.

map_quality.rationale: The answer provides a grouped structure covering the right areas, but each group contains only one file — clearly a thin sub-sample rather than a complete map. Critical model-layer pieces are missing (app/models/optimized_image.rb is absent despite being a first-order edit target per the contract), and the dependent list drops whole clusters: lib/s3_inventory.rb, lib/pretty_text.rb secure-upload rewriting, lib/site_setting_extension.rb, topic-links, hotlinked-image/video jobs, lib/email/styles.rb, site-icon/splash/manifest theming paths, user-merger, and plugin-register-upload; a teammate could not land the change from this map alone.

specificity.rationale: app/models/upload.rb, has_url.rb, and cooked_post_processor.rb:143 are concrete, but app/models/theme_setting.rb:3, app/models/user_profile.rb:3, and app/services/site_setting/update.rb:3 all land on line 3 — a tell-tale placeholder rather than a real insertion point. The three-file spec list has no file:line and omits spec/lib coverage for email, backup, S3, and job paths entirely.

justification.rationale: Area headings ("Email", "Backup and restore") provide implicit category context, but no file in the map has a stated reason for its inclusion — what the Upload contract change does to it, which association or path triggers the dependency, or why it is at risk. It is a labelled list, not a justification.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags zero uncertainty — no caveat on whether line numbers are accurate, no acknowledgement that indirect/concern-reached dependents may be missing, no note on storage-semantic assumptions or fixture completeness. An agent reading this would act with false confidence on an incomplete map.

relationship.rationale: No connecting relationship is stated for any dependent: the map does not say that cooked_post_processor reaches Upload through post associations and URL rewriting, that s3_store is the storage-abstraction delegate, that backup/creator bundles remote uploads via URL resolution, or which spec exercises which contract path. It is a grouped file list; the routes linking each entry to the Upload contract change are entirely absent.
